pr.title: T-03: JWT authentication with Spring Security 6 + multi-tenant context
pr.body:
## Summary

Implements JWT-based authentication with Spring Security 6, refresh token flow, and per-request tenant isolation via ThreadLocal.

### What's included:

| Component | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| **Dependencies** | `spring-boot-starter-security`, `spring-security-test`, JJWT 0.12.3 (api + impl + jackson) |
| **Flyway V3** | `refresh_tokens` table (token, user_id, expires_at 7d, CASCADE on user delete) |
| **JPA Entities** | `Tenant`, `User`, `Client`, `RefreshToken` — mapped to existing V1/V3 schema |
| **JwtService** | Generates access tokens (24h TTL) with userId/tenantId/role claims; validates signature via `JWT_SECRET` |
| **AuthService** | Login (BCrypt verify), refresh (validate + reissue), logout (delete refresh token) |
| **TenantContext** | ThreadLocal holding current tenant UUID — set by JwtFilter, cleared in finally block |
| **JwtFilter** | `OncePerRequestFilter` — extracts Bearer token, validates JWT, sets SecurityContext + TenantContext |
| **SecurityConfig** | `/api/auth/**` + `/api/health` + `/actuator/**` = public. `/api/**` = JWT required. Stateless sessions. 401 JSON on auth failure. |
| **AuthController** | `POST /api/auth/login` → access + refresh token (httpOnly cookie). `POST /api/auth/refresh` → new access token from cookie. `POST /api/auth/logout` → delete refresh token + clear cookie. |
| **ClientController** | `GET /api/clients` — returns only clients belonging to the authenticated user's tenant |
| **GlobalExceptionHandler** | Maps `AuthException` → 401 JSON |
| **Config** | `jwt.secret` in application.yml (from `JWT_SECRET` env var), test profile includes a test secret |

### Files created (22 new, 3 modified):

**New:**
- `V3__refresh_tokens.sql` — migration
- `Tenant.java`, `User.java`, `Client.java`, `RefreshToken.java` — entities
- `TenantRepository`, `UserRepository`, `ClientRepository`, `RefreshTokenRepository` — repositories
- `JwtService.java`, `AuthService.java` — services
- `TenantContext.java`, `JwtFilter.java`, `SecurityConfig.java` — security
- `AuthController.java`, `ClientController.java`, `GlobalExceptionHandler.java` — controllers
- `LoginRequest.java`, `LoginResponse.java`, `TokenResponse.java`, `ClientResponse.java` — DTOs
- `MultiTenantAuthTest.java` — integration test (6 test cases)

**Modified:**
- `pom.xml` — security + JJWT dependencies
- `application.yml` — `jwt.secret` property
- `application-test.yml` — test JWT secret

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] **Multi-tenant isolation**: Log in as the demo admin (`admin@demo.com` / `Admin123!`), call `GET /api/clients` with the access token. Verify it only returns clients from the demo tenant and not from other tenants.
- [ ] **Auth flow end-to-end**: Test login → get tokens → use access token → refresh → logout → verify token no longer works
- [ ] **401 enforcement**: Call `GET /api/clients` without a token and verify you get `{"error":"Unauthorized"}`
- [ ] **Health endpoint still public**: Verify `GET /api/health` returns `{"status":"ok"}` without any token

**Test plan:**
1. Start with Docker: `docker compose down -v && docker compose up --build`
2. Login: `curl -X POST http://localhost:8085/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"admin@demo.com","password":"Admin123!"}'`
3. Use the returned `accessToken` to call: `curl http://localhost:8085/api/clients -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'`
4. Verify health still works without auth: `curl http://localhost:8085/api/health`
5. Verify 401 without token: `curl http://localhost:8085/api/clients`

### Notes

- The `JWT_SECRET` env var must be at least 32 characters. A default dev-only secret is provided for local development.
- Refresh tokens are stored in the DB (not Redis) with 7-day TTL, returned as httpOnly cookie + in the login response body.
- The `CorsFilter` bean from the existing `CorsConfig` is injected into the Spring Security filter chain to ensure CORS headers are applied before auth checks.
- All 6 unit tests pass: multi-tenant client isolation for both tenants, 401 without token, health endpoint public access, wrong password rejection.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/28f22dd6a7d44cc8b0cd820ac982a0cf
Requested by: @ranganvaradan
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
